title: terebinthus pannosa
intro: Terebinthus pannosa, also known as the sandalwood spurge or sandalwood tree, is a plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of resinous exudates, which contain compounds like alpha-pinene and beta-pinene, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Terebinthus pannosa include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and combat infections, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Terebinthus pannosa can be obtained through steam distillation of the plant's resin or roots, resulting in essential oils and extracts used in skincare products and traditional medicine.
